skill.name: ckm:banner-design
skill.description: Design banners for social media, ads, website heroes, creative assets, and print. Multiple art direction options with AI-generated visuals. Actions: design, create, generate banner. Platforms: Facebook, Twitter/X, LinkedIn, YouTube, Instagram, Google Display, website hero, print. Styles: minimalist, gradient, bold typography, photo-based, illustrated, geometric, retro, glassmorphism, 3D, neon, duotone, editorial, collage. Uses ui-ux-pro-max, frontend-design, ai-artist, ai-multimodal skills.
skill.summary: Create platform-ready banners across social, web, and print with multiple art direction options and AI-generated visuals.
skill.author: nextlevelbuilder

security_audit.summary: Static scanner flagged 90 patterns (high external_commands, network, and weak crypto categories), but manual review confirms all findings are false positives. The 'backtick execution' patterns are markdown code blocks documenting legitimate bash/python/node commands for AI image generation and screenshot export. The 'weak cryptographic algorithm' detections are spurious matches in markdown text with no actual crypto code. The two hardcoded URLs reference localhost for local dev server, and hidden file paths reference the .claude skills directory. No prompt injection, data exfiltration, or malicious intent detected.

low_findings.title: Documentation references to external command execution
low_findings.description: The skill contains markdown code blocks showing bash, python3, and node commands for invoking AI image generation (gemini_batch_process.py) and screenshot tools (screenshot.js). These are documentation examples, not executable code in the skill itself. User review of the actual executed commands is recommended before running them.

low_findings.title: Documentation references to localhost URLs
low_findings.description: Two instances of http://localhost:8765 in example commands for local dev server testing. These are not network exfiltration vectors; they reference local development workflows.

content.value_statement: Creating platform-specific banners requires precise dimensions, safe zones, and art direction choices. This skill provides a guided workflow that produces multiple banner design options with correct specs for social, web, and print.
actual_capabilities: Generates multiple art direction options per banner request using predefined style guidelines
Provides platform-specific dimensions and safe zones for Facebook, Twitter/X, LinkedIn, YouTube, Instagram, Google Display, and website heroes
Creates HTML/CSS banner layouts that respect typography, contrast, and CTA placement rules
Invokes AI image generation scripts (gemini_batch_process.py) for background visuals and hero illustrations
Exports final banners to PNG using chrome-devtools screenshot scripts at exact platform dimensions
Applies print specifications including 300 DPI, CMYK color mode, and 3-5mm bleed
limitations: Does not handle video editing, full website design, or production print setup
Requires ai-artist, ai-multimodal, and chrome-devtools skills to be installed for image generation and export
Visual generation quality depends on external Gemini API availability and quotas
Brand context injection requires docs/brand-guidelines.md to exist in the user project

use_cases.title: Social media campaign banners
use_cases.description: Marketing teams can quickly produce banner sets for product launches across Facebook, Instagram, LinkedIn, and Twitter/X with correct dimensions for each platform.
use_cases.target_user: Marketing managers and social media coordinators

use_cases.title: Website hero section design
use_cases.description: Web designers can generate multiple hero banner concepts with different art directions, then export them at the right resolution for production use.

use_cases.title: Display ad creative production
use_cases.description: Ad designers can create variations for Google Display Network sizes (medium rectangle, leaderboard, mobile banner) with consistent branding.

prompt_templates.title: Basic social media banner
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a minimalist banner for our LinkedIn company page. Dimensions 1584x396. Headline: 'Build Faster Together'. CTA: 'Start Free Trial'. Use our brand colors (see docs/brand-guidelines.md).
prompt_templates.scenario: First-time user requesting a single banner

prompt_templates.title: Multi-variant ad campaign
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate 3 banner options for a Google Display ad campaign promoting our SaaS product. Sizes: 300x250, 728x90, 160x600. Try minimalist, bold typography, and gradient styles. Headline: 'Ship Code 10x Faster'. CTA: 'Try Free'.
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate user creating A/B test variants

prompt_templates.title: Event promotion banner set
prompt_templates.prompt: Create banners for a tech conference. Platform: Twitter/X header (1500x500) and Instagram story (1080x1920). Style: neon/cyberpunk. Theme: 'Future of AI 2026'. Date and venue in supporting text. QR code in bottom-right for ticket purchase.

prompt_templates.title: Brand-consistent hero refresh
prompt_templates.prompt: Refresh our website hero (1920x600) with photo-based art direction. Match existing brand: navy blue, white, accent orange. Show our product in use, lifestyle imagery. Headline: 'Data Security Made Simple'. Subtext: 'Enterprise-grade protection for modern teams'.
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced user with established brand guidelines

output_examples.input: Design 3 banner options for a SaaS product launch on Facebook (820x312) and Instagram post (1080x1080). Styles: minimalist, bold typography, gradient.
output: Minimalist variant: Clean white background, navy logo top-left, 'Launch Faster' in 48px sans-serif, single CTA button bottom-right
Bold typography variant: Full-bleed gradient background, 'LAUNCH' in 120px display font as hero element, subtext in 18px
Gradient variant: Mesh gradient background (purple to pink), 16px body text, 'Try Free' CTA with high contrast
All variants: exported as PNG at exact platform dimensions, saved to assets/banners/{campaign}/

output_examples.input: Generate a Twitter/X header (1500x500) for a tech newsletter. Style: editorial. Theme: 'Weekly AI Insights'.
output: Left column: newsletter logo and issue number
Center column: 'WEEKLY AI INSIGHTS' pull quote in serif typeface
Right column: subscribe URL and QR code
Safe zone respected: critical content in central 70% of 1500x500 canvas

output_examples.input: Create a print roll-up banner (850mm x 2000mm) for a trade show. Brand: eco-friendly packaging startup. Style: nature/organic.
output: Top zone: logo and tagline 'Packaging That Grows Back'
Middle zone: hero photo of sustainable materials, botanical motifs in earth tones
Bottom zone: CTA with QR code to website, social handles
Print specs: 300 DPI, CMYK color, 3mm bleed all sides, exported at production resolution

best_practices: Always provide brand context via docs/brand-guidelines.md before generating banners to ensure color and typography consistency
Generate 2-3 art direction options per request to give stakeholders real choices rather than a single concept
Respect platform safe zones: keep critical content in the central 70-80% of the canvas to avoid UI chrome overlap
Use the Pro model (gemini-3-pro-image-preview) for hero illustrations and the Standard model (gemini-2.5-flash-image) for backgrounds and patterns to balance quality and speed
anti_patterns: Do not exceed the 20% text-to-image ratio on Meta ad banners, as the platform penalizes heavy text with reduced reach
Do not use more than 2 typefaces per banner, as mixed typography reduces readability and brand consistency
Do not place CTAs in platform UI overlay areas (e.g., within 100px of edges on mobile stories), as users will not be able to tap them

faq.question: Which platforms are supported for banner sizes?
faq.answer: The skill includes preset dimensions for Facebook, Twitter/X, LinkedIn, YouTube, Instagram, Google Display Network, website heroes, and print formats. You can also request custom dimensions.

faq.question: How many art direction options are generated per request?
faq.answer: The default is 3 options per request. You can ask for 2-5 depending on your needs. Each option is produced in a distinct style from the 22 available art directions.

faq.question: Does the skill generate images directly?
faq.answer: No. The skill orchestrates the workflow but relies on the ai-artist and ai-multimodal skills to invoke Gemini image generation. You need those skills installed.

faq.question: What image quality can I expect?
faq.answer: The Standard model produces 2K images suitable for backgrounds and patterns. The Pro model produces 4K images suitable for hero illustrations and product shots.

faq.question: Can I export banners at exact platform dimensions?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill uses the chrome-devtools screenshot script to export HTML banners as PNG at the exact width and height you specify.

faq.question: Is brand guideline support available?
faq.answer: Yes. If docs/brand-guidelines.md exists in your project, the skill reads it and injects brand context (colors, fonts, voice) into the generated designs.
